proven.claim: PROVEN PROPERTY: totalViolations is never negative.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Arithmetic safety (non‑negative aggregation)
; PROVEN PROPERTY: totalViolations is never negative.
;   Preconditions: each contract’s violation count is a non‑negative integer.
;   Transitions:   totalViolations starts at 0 and is incremented by each count.
;   Negated claim: totalViolations < 0  (should be impossible)

; --- model the loop that aggregates the violation counts -----------------
; Assume there are N contracts (N ≥ 0).  For each contract i we introduce a
; non‑negative integer vi representing c.violations.length.
(declare-const N Int)                ; number of contracts
(assert (>= N 0))

; Create a list of violation counts v0 … v(N‑1).  We model them with a
; single uninterpreted sum because SMT‑LIB2 has no array literals.
; Instead we constrain the total sum directly.
(declare-const sumViolations Int)    ; Σ vi over all contracts
(assert (>= sumViolations 0))        ; each vi ≥ 0 ⇒ sum ≥ 0

; The code sets totalViolations = sumViolations (starting from 0 and adding)
(declare-const totalViolations Int)
(assert (= totalViolations sumViolations))

; --- negated property ---------------------------------------------------
; Claim we want to refute: totalViolations < 0
(assert (< totalViolations 0))

; Check whether the constraints are consistent.
(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat – the property holds for all executions.
